FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Slider

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI 3 Slider. Geometry (Slider_themeresources.xaml): a 4px rail (SliderTrackFill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault, radius 2) with an accent value-fill, and the Fluent thumb — a STATIC 22px ring (the 18px Thumb part + its Border's −2 margin, lines 169-172 + 198) of ControlSolidFillColorDefault with a 1px ControlElevationBorder, holding the only animated part: the 12px accent inner dot whose storyboards scale it 0.86 at rest / 1.167 on hover / 0.71 on press / 1.167 disabled (lines 204-254). Value-fill and inner dot recolor with the CONTROL-wide PointerOver/Pressed states (AccentFillColorDefault → Secondary → Tertiary; lines 14-17, 24-27).

  • Create(float, Action<float>, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)/Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) — the lightweight 0..1 scrub primitives (media seek/volume). Keyboard, focus visuals and the optional header are included; the value tooltip is not (see Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?)).
  • Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the full WinUI-parity control over Slider.Options: arbitrary range, step snapping, ticks (incl. inline), Header, SmallChange/LargeChange keyboard, and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled "disambiguation UI" value tooltip with a ThumbToolTipValueConverter-equivalent formatter.

Keyboard (KeyPress::Slider::KeyDown, SliderKeyProcess.h:28-71): Left/Down decrease and Right/Up increase by SmallChange, Home/End jump to Minimum/Maximum; PageUp/PageDown move by LargeChange (not in XAML's KeyPress::Slider — kept per the parity contract, riding the same Slider::Step math, Slider_Partial.cpp:1713-1783). RTL note: WinUI inverts Left/Right by (FlowDirection==RightToLeft) ^ IsDirectionReversed (SliderKeyProcess.h:26); the engine has no FlowDirection yet — the LTR mapping ships now, the inversion lands with the Wave-6 RTL sweep.

public static class Slider

Fields

PartContainer

The interactive full-size track surface (WinUI SliderContainer): the hit target carrying the scrub + keyboard (and, on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?), tooltip) mechanics. Owned: OnPointerDown, OnDrag, OnKeyDown, Role, Children — and on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) also OnRealized (track ref, chained) plus the tooltip wiring (OnClick/OnHoverMove/OnPointerExit/OnFocusChanged) while the tooltip is enabled.

public const string PartContainer = "Container"

Field Value

string

PartInnerDot

The 12px animated accent dot inside the thumb (WinUI SliderInnerThumb — the only animated thumb part). Owned: the storyboard scale ramp — ScaleX/ScaleY (the rest/disabled base), HoverScale, PressScale; colors, size and durations stay restylable.

public const string PartInnerDot = "InnerDot"

Field Value

string

PartRail

The 4px resting rail (WinUI HorizontalTrackRect / VerticalTrackRect). Owned: nothing — pure styling (the hover/press fill pins are stock state styling a modifier may override).

public const string PartRail = "Rail"

Field Value

string

PartThumb

The static 22px thumb ring (WinUI HorizontalThumb / VerticalThumb). Owned: Children (the inner dot), OnRealized (the Ranged tooltip anchor, chained), TransformBind on the Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) path, and the value-position OffsetX/OffsetY on the vertical Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) path.

public const string PartThumb = "Thumb"

Field Value

string

PartTickBar

Every tick bar on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) (WinUI TopTickBar/BottomTickBar/HorizontalInlineTickBar — one part name, applied to every mounted instance). Owned: Children (the marks sit at value positions).

public const string PartTickBar = "TickBar"

Field Value

string

PartValueFill

The accent value fill (WinUI HorizontalDecreaseRect / VerticalDecreaseRect). Owned: the value-position geometry — Width (horizontal) / Height+OffsetY (vertical) on the layout paths; Width+TransformBind on the signal-bound Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) path (the grow-from-left math assumes the full track Width).

public const string PartValueFill = "ValueFill"

Field Value

string

StyleOverride 

public static Slider.Style? StyleOverride

Field Value

Slider.Style

Properties

DefaultStyle 

public static Slider.Style DefaultStyle { get; }

Property Value

Slider.Style

Methods

Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)

Signals-native slider (the "even better than React" path): the value is a FloatSignal bound straight to the value-fill's composited ScaleX and the thumb's composited OffsetX. A drag writes the signal, which updates exactly those two node transforms on the compositor fast path — zero render, zero reconcile, zero relayout per pointer-move (the slider-tank fix). onChange is invoked for side effects (e.g. a value readout that takes its own scoped re-render); pass null for a purely-visual scrub. Keyboard steps write the signal the same compositor-only way. No value tooltip: opening/observing one requires a re-render path, which would break this overload's zero-re-render contract — use Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) for the WinUI-parity surface.

public static BoxEl Bind(FloatSignal value, Action<float>? onChange = null, float width = 200, float height = 24, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, string? header = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value FloatSignal
onChange Action<float>
width float
height float
style Slider.Style
isEnabled bool
header string
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Create(float, Action<float>, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)

The minimal 0..1 controlled slider (media seek/volume). Press-to-seek + drag-to-scrub with the raw x/width mapping, full keyboard (SmallChange 0.01 / LargeChange 0.1 — WinUI's defaults 1/10 re-based from its 0–100 default range, Slider_Partial.h:13-15), focus ring at FocusVisualMargin −7,0,−7,0 (Slider_themeresources.xaml:184), optional header. No value tooltip (that lives on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?), the WinUI-parity surface).

public static BoxEl Create(float value, Action<float> onChange, float width = 200, float height = 24, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, string? header = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value float
onChange Action<float>
width float
height float
style Slider.Style
isEnabled bool
header string
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

FormatThumbValue(float, float)

The default thumb-tooltip text — DefaultDisambiguationUIConverter (Slider_Partial.cpp:1859-1936): the number of decimal places follows StepFrequency's mantissa, capped at 4; the value is rounded to that digit. A continuous slider (step ≤ 0) formats like WinUI's default StepFrequency = 1 → no decimals.

public static string FormatThumbValue(float value, float step)

Parameters

value float
step float

Returns

string

Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?)

A slider over an arbitrary Min..Max range, with step snapping, tick marks (incl. inline), vertical orientation, Header, the full WinUI keyboard map and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled value tooltip. value is in range units; length is the track length (px) and thickness the cross size (WinUI SliderHorizontalHeight/SliderVerticalWidth = 32, Slider_themeresources.xaml:167-168). Controlled: the caller owns the value. Internally a stateful component (the tooltip's overlay lifetime spans re-renders); props are pushed through a context provider — the engine's sanctioned prop channel for embedded components.

public static BoxEl Ranged(float value, Action<float> onChange, Slider.Options o, float length = 220, float thickness = 32, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value float
onChange Action<float>
o Slider.Options
length float
thickness float
style Slider.Style
isEnabled bool
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
